content.title: Copilot Cowork: A new way of getting work done | Microsoft 365 Blog
content.text:
Now you can hand that triage over to Cowork. It reviews your Outlook schedule, asks what you are trying to prioritize, and flags conflicts and low-value meetings. Then it proposes changes.

Copilot Cowork is currently being tested with a limited set of customers in Research Preview, and it will be more broadly available in the Frontier program in late March 2026. Learn how to power your Frontier Transformation with Copilot and agents. ... Charles leads the design, product development, and engineering teams responsible for creating intelligent business applications and low-code platforms with generative AI. This spans products and services across Microsoft Dynamics 365, Power Platform, Copilot Studio, Copilot apps, and Nuance Enterprise. These products include business applications for Microsoft Copilot, low-code/no-code tools, industry cloud solutions, conversational AI, RPA, CRM, and ERP applications.

Copilot Cowork is built for that: it helps Copilot take action, not just chat. Cowork makes it easy to delegate work. Describe the outcome you want and Cowork automatically grounds the work in your emails, meetings, messages, files, and data. Powered by Work IQ, Cowork draws on signals across Outlook, Teams, Excel, and the rest of Microsoft 365 so it can act with the same understanding you bring to your job. When you hand off a task to Cowork, it turns your request into a plan. The plan continues in the background, with clear checkpoints so you can confirm progress, make changes, or pause execution at any time. Cowork checks in if it needs clarification. You can see any actions that it is recommending, then approve changes before they are applied. Copilot works independently without you giving up control. In the last few weeks, what has stood out most is how naturally Cowork fits into a busy day.

It is easy to have a dozen tasks in flight at once, each one moving forward while you focus on what only you can do. The era of Copilot execution is here. ... That plan-to-action loop is the difference between getting an answer and getting something done. Here are four examples of Cowork turning intent into actions across Microsoft 365. Each starts with a simple ask and ends with actions that stay in your control. Across each scenario, Cowork is not just creating content—it is coordinating the work around it. Most weeks start with a packed calendar and not enough focus time. Now you can hand that triage over to Cowork. It reviews your Outlook schedule, asks what you are trying to prioritize, and flags conflicts and low-value meetings. Then it proposes changes. Once you approve, it applies the changes by accepting, declining, or rescheduling meetings and adding focus blocks.

Cowork builds a competitive comparison in Excel, distills differentiation into a value proposition document, and generates a customer pitch deck. It can also outline milestones, owners, and next steps. This does not stop at strategy. It translates into coordinated action. You get a coherent story quickly, plus the files to back it up, without stitching together versions across tools. From there, your team can distribute it, review it, and continue improving as the launch evolves. Copilot Cowork runs within Microsoft 365’s security and governance boundaries. Identity, permissions, and compliance policies apply by default, and actions and outputs are auditable. Cowork runs in a protected, sandboxed cloud environment, so tasks can keep progressing safely as you move across devices. This is what makes execution durable at enterprise scale. Working closely with Anthropic, we have integrated the technology behind Claude Cowork into Microsoft 365 Copilot.

content.title: Microsoft 365 AI: Copilot Cowork Redefines Work Automation
content.text:
Microsoft demonstrated four real-world use cases at launch: Calendar cleanup - Cowork reviews your Outlook schedule, flags conflicts and low-value meetings, proposes changes, and once approved, accepts, declines, or reschedules meetings and ...

AI that answers questions is useful. AI that gets work done is transformational. On March 9, 2026, Microsoft introduced Copilot Cowork - and it marks one of the most significant shifts in how enterprise teams will interact with AI inside Microsoft 365. ... Copilot Cowork is built for taking action, not just chatting. Describe the outcome you want, and Cowork automatically grounds the work in your emails, meetings, messages, files, and data. Powered by Work IQ, Cowork draws on signals across Outlook, Teams, Excel, and the rest of Microsoft 365 so it can act with the same understanding you bring to your job. This capability brings long-running, multi-step work into Microsoft 365 Copilot, moving beyond prompts and responses toward execution that unfolds over time. Instead of asking Copilot to generate a single artifact, Cowork allows you to delegate meaningful work and stay in the loop as that work progresses ·

Copilot Cowork is currently available in a limited Research Preview to select customers, with a broader rollout scheduled through the Frontier program in late March 2026. Microsoft plans to make the Microsoft 365 E7 suite generally available on May 1 for $99 per user per month, with E7 unifying E5, M365 Copilot, Agent 365, and other parts of Microsoft's product portfolio under a single plan - the first new enterprise license plan in approximately 10 years. Cowork is built with enterprise needs in mind - work is observable, actions are transparent, and everything operates within Microsoft's security, identity, and governance framework so organizations can adopt these capabilities with confidence. Targeted at enterprise and business professionals, Copilot Cowork focuses on automating meeting management, research, and cross-functional project coordination.

When you hand off a task to Cowork, it turns your request into a plan. The plan continues in the background, with clear checkpoints so you can confirm progress, make changes, or pause execution at any time. Cowork checks in if it needs clarification. You can see any actions it is recommending, then approve changes before they are applied - Copilot works independently without you giving up control. Microsoft demonstrated four real-world use cases at launch: Calendar cleanup - Cowork reviews your Outlook schedule, flags conflicts and low-value meetings, proposes changes, and once approved, accepts, declines, or reschedules meetings and adds focus blocks. Meeting preparation - It aggregates inputs from emails, past meetings, and files to produce a full meeting packet: a briefing document, supporting analysis, a client-ready deck, and a pre-scheduled prep block on the calendar.

content.title: Microsoft Copilot Cowork Marshals Corporate Intelligence, AI to Execute Complex Tasks - Cloud Wars
content.text:
During an online event, Charles Lamanna, President, Business Applications and Agents and Microsoft, demoed Copilot Cowork performing a range of work in preparation for a customer meeting. “I want to get all the emails, the meetings, the notes that are relevant, and then I want to create a presentation, and I want to go have an Excel overview of product growth and have all that information just generate for me automatically,” he said. “What’s great about Cowork is it can run for hours, if that’s how long it takes.” · In addition to the demo of building a meeting packet and aligning team members in meeting prep, specific use cases for Cowork cited by Microsoft included: ... By combining Anthropic’s agentic model in Claude Cowork for multi-step tasks with Microsoft 365, Cowork delivers a managed experience combining AI-powered reasoning with enterprise-level controls.

Microsoft this week introduced a series of new and enhanced Copilot features led by “Copilot Cowork,” based on Anthropic Claude Cowork technology, for automating and managing complex tasks across workflows. The company’s Copilot Wave 3 also includes Microsoft 365 Copilot built natively into the core productivity apps Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook. At a Monday online event, the company disclosed general availability of the previously announced Agent 365 “control plane” for management and governance. The company’s event also included rollout of Microsoft 365 E7, “the Frontier Suite” combining Microsoft 365 E5, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Agent 365, and security functionality. Copilot Cowork automates work across multiple steps and applications, leveraging Microsoft’s WorkIQ intelligence layer to reason over all relevant materials for full work context.

At the same time, Copilot Cowork and an expanding alliance with Anthropic — including Claude technology powering new SharePoint functionality — continues to advance Microsoft’s stated commitment to model choice. “We have the ability to reach across the industry and look at innovations coming from OpenAI, look at those coming out of Anthropic and other great partners, bring them into this system that we call copilot and agents that allows us to do something that’s really unique,” said Jared Spataro, Chief Marketing Officer, AI@Work at Microsoft. With Wave 3, Claude and the latest OpenAI models are both available in Copilot via the Frontier program; Copilot automatically applies the right model for the task. “There’s lots of great models out there. There’s also a lot of great agent harnesses that take the models and kind of execute them.

Copilot Cowork will be available through the Frontier program in late March. Copilot Wave 3 embeds Microsoft 365 Copilot natively in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook. WorkIQ keeps Copilot grounded in work context, so updates always reflect what is current across files, meetings, and chats. The online event featured a demo of Copilot in Excel. “This is native to Excel. It’s creating the right structure. It’s inserting real Excel formulas. It speaks Excel. Every change is transparent, it’s auditable, it’s reviewable, and its reversible,” said Sumit Chauhan, Corporate Vice President, Office Product Group at Microsoft. These new Copilot experiences are generally available in Excel and Word, with PowerPoint and Outlook starting to roll out over the coming months. In Copilot Wave 3, chat in Copilot becomes the entry point for content creation and task execution.

content.title: Microsoft announces Copilot Cowork with help from Anthropic — a cloud-powered AI agent that works across M365 apps | VentureBeat
content.text:
Like Claude Cowork, Copilot Cowork users to delegate complex, multi-step tasks to an AI agent that plans, executes, and delivers finished work — in this case, the AI is able to move across and use all the tools and features of Microsoft's ...

This morning, Microsoft announced "Copilot Cowork" a new cloud-based AI agentic automation tool within Microsoft's existing AI tool 365 Copilot, except now it can complete work on users' behalf across many Microsoft apps, instead of contained within each one. If it sounds suspiciously similar to Anthropic's own "Claude Cowork" applications for Mac and Windows (released in January and February of 2026, respectively) that's to be expected — as Microsoft and Anthropic worked together on this new feature. Copilot Cowork is the centerpiece of what Microsoft is calling "Wave 3" of Microsoft 365 Copilot — a sweeping platform update that also brings agentic capabilities directly into individual Office apps, makes Anthropic's Claude models available in mainline Copilot Chat, and introduces new enterprise pricing tiers designed to bundle AI productivity with security and governance.

Copilot Cowork operates in the cloud, inside Microsoft 365's infrastructure, and draws on something Claude Cowork simply cannot access: the full graph of a user's enterprise work data.

In each case, Microsoft stressed that Cowork isn't just creating content — it's coordinating the work around it, producing multiple connected deliverables across applications in a single workflow. This is the clearest public confirmation yet that Microsoft's deepening relationship with Anthropic — the $30 billion Azure compute deal announced in November 2025, the integration of Claude Opus 4.6 into Microsoft Foundry in early February 2026, the ongoing internal adoption of Claude Code across Microsoft engineering teams, according to The Verge — has now reached the company's flagship productivity suite. But it goes beyond Cowork. According to Spataro's companion blog post, Claude is now available in mainline Copilot Chat for Frontier program users, alongside the latest generation of OpenAI models. That means Anthropic's models aren't just powering Cowork's task execution behind the scenes — they're becoming a general-purpose option that users can access directly in everyday Copilot conve

Copilot Cowork is currently in Research Preview with a limited set of customers. Broader access will come through Microsoft's Frontier program in late March 2026. Enterprises interested in getting early access can join the Frontier program at adoption.microsoft.com/en-us/copilot/frontier-program/. Microsoft also published a companion blog post, "Powering Frontier Transformation with Copilot and agents," that outlines how organizations can prepare for the rollout. The announcement represents Microsoft's most significant step yet in transforming Copilot from a conversational assistant into what the company calls an execution layer — an AI that doesn't just answer questions but actually completes work on a user's behalf. But with Claude Cowork offering much of the same functionality — the question is whether Microsoft's first-party offering comes with enough unique advantages or integration with trusted systems currently used by enterprises, to catch on.

Microsoft has introduced Copilot Cowork, a new AI-powered feature embedded within Microsoft 365 that moves beyond conversational assistance to autonomous task execution. Unlike traditional Copilot interactions focused on answering questions or drafting content, Cowork is designed to delegate, plan, and complete multi-step workflows across the entire Microsoft 365 ecosystem while keeping users firmly in control. Powered by Work IQ, Cowork draws real-time signals from Outlook, Teams, Excel, and other Microsoft 365 applications to ground every task in the user’s actual work context. Users describe an intended outcome, and Cowork builds an actionable plan that runs in the background. At key checkpoints, it surfaces progress updates, flags areas needing clarification, and requires approval before applying any changes, ensuring autonomous execution without sacrificing oversight.

Microsoft also disclosed a notable architectural decision: Cowork integrates technology developed in collaboration with Anthropic, embedding the Claude model into Microsoft 365 Copilot’s multi-model infrastructure. This multi-model approach allows Copilot to dynamically select the most capable model for a given task, regardless of its origin, a strategy Microsoft describes as a core differentiator against single-model AI platforms. Cowork is currently available through the Microsoft 365 Copilot Frontier Program, an early access initiative for enterprise customers seeking to adopt the latest Copilot capabilities before general availability. Organizations interested in evaluating Cowork can join the Frontier Program through Microsoft’s adoption portal. The launch signals a broader industry shift: enterprise AI is rapidly evolving from passive assistant to active collaborator, capable of coordinating complex, cross-functional work while operating within clearly defined security and govern

Therefore, the design aims to balance automation with transparency and human control. The video highlights practical scenarios where Copilot Cowork can add value, such as optimizing calendars and preparing meetings.

Autonomy with oversight and enterprise controls: Cowork works independently but provides checkpoints, visible actions, and pause/steer options. Microsoft adds enterprise-grade controls to prevent unintended changes and protect data. Copilot Wave 3 and March 9, 2026: Microsoft announced Cowork as part of Copilot Wave 3 on March 9, 2026. Wave 3 also expanded SharePoint grounding, improved agent coordination, and tightened continuous Copilot workflows across Microsoft 365. Lisa Crosbie [MVP] published a YouTube video that explains Microsoft’s new Copilot feature and its implications for everyday work. In her clear walkthrough, she focuses on the recent March 9, 2026 announcement that Microsoft calls Copilot Wave 3 and introduces a new mode named Copilot Cowork. Consequently, this article summarizes the video’s key points and assesses what the change means for users and organizations alike.

According to the video, Copilot Cowork converts user requests into structured plans and executes those plans in the background using an intelligence layer Microsoft calls Work IQ. The system sequences tasks, reasons across multiple apps such as Outlook, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, and then reports visible progress so users can intervene when needed. Crosbie emphasizes that the model checks in for clarification and shows recommended actions before applying them, which preserves oversight and helps prevent unintended changes. Therefore, the design aims to balance automation with transparency and human control. The video highlights practical scenarios where Copilot Cowork can add value, such as optimizing calendars and preparing meetings. For example, it can detect scheduling conflicts, low-value meetings, and provide suggestions to accept, decline, or reschedule events while adding focus time to your calendar.

Copilot Cowork and agentic architecture: Copilot Cowork is a Microsoft 365 feature that runs multi-step tasks on your behalf. It uses agentic architecture to turn your outcome requests into ongoing background workflows you can monitor. Work IQ and multi-step execution: Cowork uses Work IQ to read signals from Outlook, Teams, files and calendars to understand context. It breaks complex jobs into sequential steps, shows progress, and asks for verification when needed. Edit with Copilot and agentic way: You can use "Edit with Copilot" inside Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook to let the agent make and suggest edits while you review. The agent presents recommended actions before applying them so you keep control. Calendar cleanup and meeting preparation: Common uses include resolving scheduling conflicts, creating focus time, and rescheduling low-value meetings after approval.

Finally, Crosbie places Copilot Cowork within a larger Wave 3 update that also enhances features such as the new Edit with Copilot mode in document apps and deeper grounding on SharePoint content. She suggests that these advances represent a trend toward more persistent, context-aware AI in the workplace, and that early adopters should pilot the tools on clear, high-value scenarios. In conclusion, the video frames the release as a meaningful shift that promises productivity gains, while also reminding viewers that governance, transparency, and careful rollout remain crucial to reap benefits safely.

Once you understand what Copilot Cowork is designed to do, the next question becomes how it actually works in day‑to‑day use. Copilot Cowork follows a consistent execution loop designed for enterprise environments. 1. Describe the outcome you want, not a step by step prompt. 2. Copilot Cowork grounds the request in your Microsoft 365 work context (e.g., emails, meetings, files, chats), known as Work IQ. 3. It converts your request into a structured plan and begins executing tasks in the background. 4. It pauses at defined checkpoints, or whenever you choose to intervene, so you can review progress, adjust direction, or stop execution. 5. It requests clarification when needed, then continues. 6. You approve recommended actions before changes are applied. This plan‑act‑approve loop is what makes Copilot Cowork practical for real business use.

LG PRI HR 맥락에 맞게 정리해드리겠습니다.

---

## Copilot Cowork로 자동화할 수 있는 업무

Cowork를 이해하는 핵심 프레임이 하나 있습니다. 기존 Copilot이 질문에 답하거나 단일 결과물을 생성하는 것이었다면, Cowork는 여러 앱에 걸쳐 다단계 업무를 실제로 실행하는 것입니다. "초안 써줘"가 아니라 "이 프로젝트 전체 준비해줘"가 가능해지는 것이죠. 작업은 단일 전환이나 단일 앱에 국한되지 않고 수 분에서 수 시간까지 실행되며, 도구와 파일을 넘나들며 조율하고 실제 결과물을 만들어냅니다.

공식적으로 발표된 네 가지 핵심 유스케이스를 HR 업무에 대입해보면 이렇습니다.

**캘린더 최적화.** Cowork가 Outlook 일정을 검토해 충돌과 저가치 회의를 표시하고, 변경안을 제안하며, 승인 후 회의 수락·거절·재조정과 집중 시간 확보를 자동 처리합니다. HR 담당자의 회의 과부하 문제에 직접 작동하는 기능입니다.

**회의 준비 패킷 자동 생성.** 이메일, 문서, 회의 노트, 캘린더를 검토해 이전 논의 요약, 관련 파일 수집, 발표 자료 초안까지 자동으로 준비합니다. 리더십 면담이나 조직 진단 회의 전 준비 업무에 바로 쓸 수 있습니다.

**신규 프로젝트 런치 플랜.** 경쟁 비교표를 Excel로 구성하고, 가치 제안 문서를 정리하고, 고객용 발표자료를 생성하며, 마일스톤과 담당자, 다음 액션까지 아웃라인을 잡습니다. EX Intelligence 같은 신규 과제 킥오프 준비에 직접 대입 가능합니다.

**거래처·이해관계자 리서치.** 웹에서 관련 뉴스와 리포트를 수집하고 핵심 정보를 요약한 후, 이메일·회의·파일에서 끌어온 정보로 발표자료를 만들어 미팅을 준비합니다.

LG PRI HR 맥락에서 가장 실질적으로 쓸 수 있는 업무를 정리하면 이렇습니다.

| 업무 유형 | Cowork 적용 방식 |
|---|---|
| 월간 Pulse Check 결과 보고 | Excel 데이터 → 분석 → Word 보고서 → PPT 요약 → 이메일 발송 준비 |
| 교육 과정 기획 | 관련 파일·이메일 수집 → 커리큘럼 초안 → 일정 제안 → 참석자 안내 초안 |
| 리더십 평가 준비 | 피평가자 관련 자료 수집 → 브리핑 문서 생성 → 면담 일정 최적화 |
| 주간 팀 업무 정리 | Teams·Outlook 신호 종합 → 주간 현황 요약 → 다음 액션 목록 |

한 가지 중요한 맥락 조건이 있습니다. Copilot Cowork는 Microsoft 365 인프라 안에서 클라우드로 작동하며, 사용자의 엔터프라이즈 업무 데이터 전체 그래프에 접근한다는 것이 Claude Cowork와의 결정적 차이입니다. LG PRI가 M365 환경을 사내에서 사용 중이라면 이 기능이 그대로 작동하지만, Google Workspace 비사용 환경임을 감안할 때 M365 라이선스 수준과 Frontier 프로그램 접근 가능 여부를 먼저 IT팀과 확인하는 것이 선행 조건입니다.

현재 Research Preview 단계이며 2026년 3월 말 Frontier 프로그램을 통해 더 넓게 제공될 예정입니다. 얼리 어답터로 파일럿을 검토할 타이밍은 지금이 적기입니다.

핵심 정보를 충분히 확인했습니다. LG PRI HR 맥락에 맞춰 설명드리겠습니다.

---

## Copilot Workflows — 개념과 작동 방식

먼저 정확한 위치를 잡는 것이 중요합니다. Workflows는 Microsoft 365 Copilot 안에 내장된 에이전트로, 자연어로 원하는 것을 설명하면 Outlook, SharePoint, Teams, Planner에 걸친 자동화 플로우를 코드 없이 생성해주는 도구입니다. Power Automate가 커넥터와 트리거를 직접 설정해야 했다면, Workflows는 그 과정을 대화로 대체합니다.

작동 원리는 단순합니다. 원하는 자동화를 자연어로 묘사하면 Workflows 에이전트가 해당 플로우를 생성하고, 비주얼 디자이너에서 검토·수정한 뒤 실제로 켜는 구조입니다. 예를 들어 "특정 SharePoint 사이트의 'Invoices' 리스트에 응답을 저장해줘" 또는 "'Global Finance Operations' 팀의 'Budget Planning' 채널에 메시지를 올려줘"처럼 사이트명이나 채널명을 그대로 말하면 됩니다.

---

## HR 업무에서 실제로 쓸 수 있는 자동화 시나리오

트리거(시작 이벤트)와 액션(실행할 작업)의 조합으로 구성되며, 이메일 수신, 일정, Teams 메시지 등 다양한 이벤트를 트리거로 사용할 수 있습니다. LG PRI HR 업무에 대입하면 이렇게 됩니다.

**Pulse Check 관련.** "매월 첫째 주 월요일에 Teams 채널에 설문 링크 공지를 자동 발송하고, 응답이 수집되면 SharePoint 리스트에 저장해줘" — 참여율 85% KPI를 위한 리마인더 자동화가 이 한 문장으로 만들어집니다.

**교육 운영 관련.** "AX 교육 신청 이메일이 오면 신청자 정보를 Planner 태스크로 생성하고, Teams 채널에 알림을 보내줘" — 수기 집계 없이 과정별 신청 현황이 실시간으로 관리됩니다.

**보고서 배포 관련.** "SharePoint의 특정 폴더에 파일이 업로드되면 팀장 그룹에게 Outlook으로 알림 메일을 자동 발송해줘" — 월간 HR 보고서 배포 플로우를 자동화할 수 있습니다.

---

## 한 가지 중요한 조건

Workflows Agent를 사용하려면 Frontier 프로그램에 가입되어 있어야 하며, Power Platform 관리 센터의 DLP 정책에 SharePoint, Approvals, Teams, Planner, Outlook 커넥터가 포함되어 있어야 합니다. 현재 Frontier 얼리 액세스 단계이므로, IT 담당자를 통해 M365 라이선스 수준과 Frontier 프로그램 접근 가능 여부를 먼저 확인하는 것이 선행 조건입니다. 이 조건이 갖춰지면 코딩 없이 HR 구성원 누구든 자연어만으로 자동화 워크플로우를 직접 만들 수 있는 환경이 됩니다. 그 자체가 Layer 2 역량 개발의 실질적 진입점이 될 수 있습니다.

content.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot이란? | Microsoft Learn
content.text:
Microsoft 365 Copilot 작업 작업에 도움이 되는 AI 기반 도구입니다. 사용자는 Copilot에 프롬프트를 입력하고 Copilot는 AI 생성 정보로 응답합니다.

Microsoft Teams, Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat 앱, Microsoft365.com 및 m365.cloud.microsoft.com Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat 사용할 수 있습니다. ... Copilot Search는 모든 Microsoft 365 애플리케이션 및 연결된 타사 데이터 원본에서 AI 기반 범용 검색 환경입니다. Microsoft 365 Copilot 통합되므로 사용자가 검색을 사용하여 필요한 결과를 찾은 다음, 더 심층 탐색 또는 후속 작업 완료를 위해 채팅으로 원활하게 전환할 수 있습니다. Copilot Search에 대해 자세히 알아보세요. ... Microsoft Graph에는 사용자, 해당 활동 및 액세스할 수 있는 organization 데이터에 대한 정보가 포함됩니다. Microsoft Graph API 사용자의 이메일, 채팅, 문서 및 모임의 정보와 같은 개인 설정된 컨텍스트를 프롬프트에 제공합니다.

Microsoft Purview는 콘텐츠의 민감도에 따라 데이터를 분류하고 레이블을 지정할 수 있습니다. 또한 무단 공유 또는 누출을 방지하고 Copilot 프롬프트 및 응답을 검토하는 데 도움이 될 수 있습니다. 자세한 내용은 Microsoft Purview 데이터 보안 및 생성 AI 앱에 대한 규정 준수 보호를 참조하세요. ... 에이전트는 AI 도우미 역할을 하며 비즈니스 프로세스를 자동화할 수 있는 범위가 지정되거나 집중된 Microsoft 365 Copilot 버전입니다. 예를 들어 지원 센터 티켓을 만드는 에이전트 또는 데이터 원본에서 직원 정보를 조회하는 인사 담당자를 만들 수 있습니다. 자세한 내용은 확장성 개요 Microsoft 365 Copilot 참조하세요. ... Microsoft 365 Copilot 위한 데이터, 개인 정보 및 보안에 대해 알아봅니다.

Microsoft 365 Copilot 작업 작업에 도움이 되는 AI 기반 도구입니다. 사용자는 Copilot에 프롬프트를 입력하고 Copilot는 AI 생성 정보로 응답합니다. 응답은 실시간으로 진행되며 사용자가 액세스할 수 있는 인터넷 기반 콘텐츠 및 작업 콘텐츠를 포함할 수 있습니다. 사용자는 작업 작업과 관련된 콘텐츠를 얻고 사용 중인 Microsoft 365 앱의 컨텍스트에서 콘텐츠를 가져옵니다. 다음 비디오에서는 Microsoft 365 Copilot 대한 개요를 제공합니다. 길이는 1분 49초입니다. 예를 들어 운영 관리자이며 인적 리소스를 사용하여 작업 설명을 업데이트하고 있다고 가정해 보겠습니다. Copilot에 기본 작업 요구 사항을 제공하여 Copilot에 작업 설명을 만들도록 요청할 수 있습니다.

Microsoft 365 Copilot 아키텍처 및 작동 방식에 대해 자세히 알아봅니다. Microsoft 365 Copilot 조직 데이터와 웹을 사용합니다. 추가 기능 라이선스가 필요합니다. Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat 웹을 사용하며 사용자는 조직 데이터를 제공할 수 있습니다. 추가 라이선스가 필요하지 않습니다. Copilot Chat 대해 자세히 알아보세요. 자세한 내용은 Microsoft 365 Copilot Copilot Chat 차이점을 참조하세요. ... 추가 라이선스가 필요하지 않은 회사 또는 교육용 Copilot 버전인 Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat 대해 자세히 알아보세요. 홈 사용자는 무료 소비자 버전인 Microsoft Copilot 자동으로 가져올 수 있습니다. 자세한 내용은 Copilot가 어떻게 도와줄 수 있나요? 를 참조하고 Windows의 Copilot에 오신 것을 환영합니다.

content.title: Microsoft Copilot 2026: How Prompt Templates and Team Sharing Transform the Beginner Experience - Windows News
content.text:
Microsoft's 2026 solution introduces a comprehensive prompt gallery with hundreds of pre-built templates categorized by application, task type, and difficulty level. These aren't simple example questions but fully structured workflows with ...

Microsoft Copilot's 2026 update transforms the AI assistant from a question-answering tool into a template-driven productivity framework. The system introduces structured prompt templates, team sharing capabilities, and deep Microsoft 365 integration that make AI assistance accessible to beginners while preserving flexibility for experts. This represents a fundamental shift toward organizational knowledge scaling through reusable AI workflows. Microsoft Copilot Microsoft 365 AI Productivity Prompt Engineering Team Collaboration Workflow Automation Enterprise AI Productivity Tools · Microsoft Copilot's 2026 evolution represents a fundamental shift from a reactive question-answering tool to a proactive productivity framework. The AI assistant now integrates deeply across Microsoft 365 applications with structured prompt templates, team collaboration features, and intelligent workflow automation that fundamentally changes how beginners approach AI-powered productivity.

For years, Microsoft Copilot users faced the daunting empty prompt box—the digital equivalent of writer's block for AI interactions. Microsoft's 2026 solution introduces a comprehensive prompt gallery with hundreds of pre-built templates categorized by application, task type, and difficulty level. These aren't simple example questions but fully structured workflows with placeholders for user-specific information. The gallery organizes templates into logical categories: "Email Drafting," "Data Analysis," "Meeting Preparation," "Document Summarization," and "Project Planning." Each template includes metadata showing success rates from other users, average time savings, and complexity ratings. A "Quick Start" section surfaces the most effective templates for new users based on their role and recent activities within Microsoft 365. Microsoft's research revealed that beginners struggled most with two aspects: knowing what Copilot could do and how to ask for it effectively.

The most significant advancement in Copilot 2026 is the team sharing infrastructure. Organizations can now create, curate, and distribute prompt templates across departments, ensuring consistency and best practices. A marketing team might develop specialized templates for campaign briefs, social media calendars, and performance reports, then share them with appropriate permissions. Team prompts appear in a dedicated section of the prompt gallery with organizational branding and version control. Managers can see which templates team members use most frequently and identify knowledge gaps. The system includes approval workflows for new templates and automatic updates when processes change. Shared prompts maintain context awareness. A sales proposal template automatically pulls in customer information from Dynamics 365, recent communication history from Outlook, and product specifications from SharePoint—all without requiring users to manually gather these resources.

When an expert employee develops an effective workflow, they can capture it as a reusable template that benefits the entire team. This prevents knowledge loss when employees change roles or leave the organization. Microsoft has implemented robust security controls around template sharing and usage. Administrators can restrict which templates are available to which user groups, require approvals for certain template categories, and audit template usage across the organization. Data handling follows Microsoft's existing compliance frameworks. Templates that process sensitive information include additional validation and approval steps. The system logs all template executions for compliance purposes while maintaining user privacy for personal productivity templates. Copilot 2026 represents a maturation of enterprise AI from experimental tool to integrated productivity layer.

일상 업무를 자동화하거나 귀하를 대신해 작업하는 에이전트를 Copilot에 추가하세요. Microsoft와 신뢰받는 파트너가 제공하는 바로 사용 가능한 에이전트가 에이전트 스토어에 준비되어 있습니다. 에이전트는 Work IQ를 활용하여 여러분의 고유한 업무 흐름과 비즈니스 요구에 맞게 최적화됩니다. ... Copilot Studio를 활용해 특정 작업이나 워크플로우에 맞는 에이전트를 직접 만들거나 사용자 지정하세요. 템플릿으로 시작하거나, 자연어를 활용해 처음부터 직접 만들 수 있습니다. ... Copilot 채팅, 파일, 회의 노트, 프로젝트 자료를 한곳에 모아 그 위에서 더 발전시켜 보세요. Copilot 전자 필기장은 콘텐츠를 정리하고 분석하고 이를 바탕으로 새로운 결과물을 만드는 데 도움을 줍니다.

Microsoft 365 Copilot과 AI 기반 에이전트가 업무를 어떻게 혁신하는지 알아보세요. 사람이 주도하고 에이전트가 운영하는 팀이 리더십을 발휘하고 자동화하며 확장할 수 있도록 지원합니다. ... 리서치 도구는 복잡한 다단계 업무 리서치를 지원하는 최초의 추론 기반 에이전트로, 업무 및 웹 데이터를 활용해 구조화된 인사이트와 보고서를 제공합니다. ... 이제 Microsoft 365 Copilot과 Copilot Studio에서 GPT-5를 사용할 수 있습니다. GPT-5는 지능 면에서 획기적인 도약을 이루었으며, 업무에 적용할 때 더 강력한 성능을 발휘합니다. ... Copilot은 엔터프라이즈급 보안과 책임 있는 AI를 기반으로 설계되어, 비즈니스에 필수적인 안전장치를 유지하면서도 더 빠르게 업무를 진행할 수 있게 해줍니다.

content.title: Get started with Workflows in Microsoft 365 Copilot (Frontier) - Microsoft Support
content.text:
Sign in to Microsoft 365 Copilot. In the left navigation pane, select Agents. Find and add Workflows Agent (Frontier). You should see Workflows in the left navigation pane under Agents. To use Workflows, your DLP policy in Power Platform admin center must include the following: Connectors: ...

Workflows is an agent in Microsoft 365 Copilot that helps you automate work across Microsoft 365 using natural language. Instead of manually configuring steps or connectors, you simply describe what you want and Workflows generates a working workflow using supported Microsoft 365 services. ... Automate tasks in apps like Outlook, SharePoint, Teams, and Planner. Trigger actions on a schedule, or in response to events. Collect and save user input with adaptive cards in Teams. Use your Microsoft 365 data—like calendar, tasks, or lists—without writing code. Test and manage workflows in a visual designer. To use Workflows Agent, you need to be part of the Frontier program. ... Workflows is currently rolling out to Frontier early access for Microsoft 365 Personal, Family, and Premium plans in select markets and languages, starting with the United States and English (en-us).

You must also include the following connectors to ensure full functionality in your organization: SharePoint, Approvals, Teams, Planner, and Outlook. Dataverse requirement: Workflows agents require Dataverse in the environment. We will add Dataverse to the environment in the case where it isn't present. Cross geo: If your environment is in a region without GPU resources, you need to toggle on the cross geo setting, Move data across region for the environment in the Power Platform admin center, allowing you to process your data outside of your geo. More information: Move data across regions for Copilots and generative AI features · Always review and test AI-generated workflows before using them in production. You can’t share workflows created in Workflows. Workflows supports a limited set of Microsoft 365 connectors; non-Microsoft and custom connectors aren’t supported. Workflows can’t fix errors in existing workflows automatically. Since Workflows relies on natural language input in ch

A trigger is an event that starts a workflow. For example, you want to get a notification in Microsoft Teams when someone sends you an email. In this case, receiving an email is the trigger that starts this workflow. ... An action is what you configure the workflow to take based on the event that causes a trigger to start. An action can be for example, to upload a file in a SharePoint site. ... For advanced workflows or to connect to non-Microsoft services, use Power Automate or List of all connectors published by non-Microsoft partners | Microsoft Learn. Admins control Workflows availability using Microsoft 365 app management settings. ... Sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center. In the left navigation pane, select Copilot > Settings > Agents. Set access to All Users or Specific users/groups as needed. Workflows follows the same governance and compliance rules as other Microsoft 365 apps.

Sign in to Microsoft 365 Copilot. In the left navigation pane, select Agents. Find and add Workflows Agent (Frontier). You should see Workflows in the left navigation pane under Agents. content.title: What’s New in Microsoft 365 Copilot | January 2026 | Microsoft Community Hub
content.text:
Users can also set up automatic replies using natural language with Copilot Chat in Outlook and the Microsoft 365 Copilot app. This feature enables users to turn automatic replies on or off, set a date range, and draft the message response leveraging their previous writing style, all over chat and without having to navigate Outlook settings. For example, users can get started using prompts like “Setup an automatic reply for next Friday,” or “Turn off my automatic replies.” This feature began rolling out in December. Users can now ground an agent on a Copilot Notebook, drawing directly from references and working materials. Grounding on a Notebook helps agents stay aligned with the context of users’ work and ensure responses remain relevant, consistent, and tailored to the information they are working with. This feature is rolling out in February.

Welcome to the January 2026 edition of What's New in Microsoft 365 Copilot! Every month, we highlight new features and enhancements to keep Microsoft 365 admins up to date with Copilot features that help your users be more productive and efficient in the apps they use every day. This month we have a special announcement to share: we’ve refreshed the Microsoft 365 Roadmap to make it easier to stay on top of the latest announcements —from core apps to Copilot and agents. Discover exciting features that are now available and coming soon, and dive deeper into the full roadmap for a comprehensive look at what’s ahead. Now let’s take a closer look at what’s new this month: ... Agent mode in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint helps users edit and refine documents, spreadsheets, and presentations by working alongside users and responding to prompts, as well as actively making changes to files while reasoning through those changes.

Users can also set up automatic replies using natural language with Copilot Chat in Outlook and the Microsoft 365 Copilot app. This feature enables users to turn automatic replies on or off, set a date range, and draft the message response leveraging their previous writing style, all over chat and without having to navigate Outlook settings. For example, users can get started using prompts like “Setup an automatic reply for next Friday,” or “Turn off my automatic replies.” This feature began rolling out in December. Users can now ground an agent on a Copilot Notebook, drawing directly from references and working materials. Grounding on a Notebook helps agents stay aligned with the context of users’ work and ensure responses remain relevant, consistent, and tailored to the information they are working with. This feature is rolling out in February.

Implicit grounding on emails and text is now available in Copilot Chat in Outlook. When users open Copilot Chat alongside an email, the email is automatically added as a grounding source in the prompt box. And if they highlight text within the email, that grounding source updates automatically to only use the highlighted text. This gives users clearer, more accurate responses because Copilot can reference the exact email content they are working with, reducing copy‑paste steps and helping them complete tasks faster and with fewer mistakes. This feature rolled out in December. Users can now take common email triage actions using natural language directly from Copilot Chat in Outlook and the Microsoft 365 Copilot app. Supported actions include marking messages as read or unread, pinning and unpinning, flagging and unflagging, and archiving messages, all designed to help users stay organized and efficient.

When creating slides with Copilot, PowerPoint can now automatically use approved enterprise assets from an organization’s SharePoint Organization Asset Library (OAL) or connected Templafy libraries. When the assets are integrated, Copilot pulls from trusted, on‑brand images while generating slides, helping teams stay consistent without extra manual work. Learn more about how to enable enterprise brand images with Copilot in PowerPoint. Users now have more control when creating presentations with Copilot in PowerPoint. During creation, they can guide the length of the deck, the narrative tone, and the overall slide style. They can also include AI‑generated images to support the content. This means they can easily tailor presentations for different audiences and purposes from the first draft. This feature rolled out in December. Microsoft Viva is expanding access to Copilot Chat Insights in Copilot Dashboard by removing the requirement for tenants to have at least 50 Microsoft 365 Copilo

content.title: Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot | Microsoft Learn
content.text:
Declarative agents enable you to configure Copilot for specific scenarios by adding custom instructions, additional knowledge, and actions to automate business processes. Because declarative agents use Copilot's AI infrastructure, model, and orchestrator, they adhere to the security, compliance, and Responsible AI (RAI) requirements for Microsoft 365. The following image shows the basic anatomy of a declarative agent. ... Custom instructions to shape Copilot's responses to your organization's specific needs or workflow. Custom knowledge to connect Microsoft 365 data sources (such as Teams messages, Copilot connectors, SharePoint, and OneDrive) or external data via Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors. Custom actions to integrate with APIs to interact with external systems in real-time. Declarative agents have the following characteristics: Hosting: Use Copilot's orchestrator and foundation model.

Extend Copilot's knowledge by integrating third‑party systems and data sources. Automate complex, multi‑step workflows across Microsoft 365 and external applications. Deliver tailored user experiences that surface where your users work—in Teams chats, Outlook, SharePoint, or custom apps. Agents enable secure, real-time interactions between Copilot and other enterprise systems. Microsoft and its partners offer a variety of prebuilt agents. You can also build your own using both low-code and pro-code tools to fit your specific requirements. For example, the Sales Agent for Copilot can automate lead management by turning contacts into sales leads within Dynamics or Salesforce, setting up meetings, and reaching out to customers, all while securely accessing Microsoft 365 and enterprise data to personalize interactions and drive business outcomes.

Declarative agents enable you to configure Copilot for specific scenarios by adding custom instructions, additional knowledge, and actions to automate business processes. Because declarative agents use Copilot's AI infrastructure, model, and orchestrator, they adhere to the security, compliance, and Responsible AI (RAI) requirements for Microsoft 365. The following image shows the basic anatomy of a declarative agent. ... Custom instructions to shape Copilot's responses to your organization's specific needs or workflow. Custom knowledge to connect Microsoft 365 data sources (such as Teams messages, Copilot connectors, SharePoint, and OneDrive) or external data via Microsoft 365 Copilot connectors. Custom actions to integrate with APIs to interact with external systems in real-time. Declarative agents have the following characteristics: Hosting: Use Copilot's orchestrator and foundation model.

Microsoft 365 Copilot is an AI-powered productivity tool that enhances workflows across Microsoft 365 applications like Copilot Chat, Outlook, Teams, and Word, using enterprise data from Microsoft Graph. Although Copilot provides powerful built-in capabilities, organizations often need to integrate additional knowledge, data sources, or applications to address specific use cases. Agents extend the functionality of Copilot by acting as specialized AI assistants tailored to specific domains. These agents apply organizational knowledge and automation to streamline business processes, enhance decision making, and improve efficiency. Agents can retrieve information, summarize data, or even take actions like sending emails or updating records. You can use one of two approaches to build agents for Copilot: the declarative approach, which utilizes your instructions, knowledge, and actions along with Copilot's orchestrator and models, or the custom engine approach, where you bring your own orch

By developing agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot, organizations can securely utilize enterprise data, take actions across applications, and enhance productivity — all within the flow of Teams, Outlook, Copilot, or other Microsoft 365 applications. ... Knowledge: Tailor the agent's responses by providing it with specialized instructions and data sources. Actions: Develop actions, triggers, and workflows that automate business processes. Beyond these core components, additional layers enhance an agent's functionality: The orchestrator acts as the central engine that manages how the agent interacts with knowledge, skills, and autonomy. The foundation models power the agent's reasoning, language understanding, and response generation, forming the intelligence layer behind every interaction. The user experience layer ensures seamless interaction between users and agents by integrating agents into Microsoft 365 applications or external platforms for an intuitive and efficient workflow.

content.title: FAQ for Workflows agent | Microsoft Learn
content.text:
To connect to services such as SharePoint or Teams, mention the site, list, or team name in your natural language prompt. For example, you can say "save the response to the 'Invoices' list on the 'ContosoCRM' SharePoint site" or "post a message ...

Workflows agent is an AI-powered assistant within Microsoft 365 Copilot that helps you create, edit, and manage automation flows across Microsoft 365 apps such as Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, and Planner. You describe what you want to automate in natural language, and Workflows agent generates the corresponding flow for you. It also helps answer questions about your flows and provides documentation links as needed. ... Create new flows from scratch based on your descriptions. Edit existing flows according to your instructions. Answer questions about the flow you’re working on. Provide descriptions of your flows in plain language. Offer helpful documentation links about Microsoft 365 automation. Filter out unrelated questions. Suggest when to save or test your flow. Workflows agent is designed to make building automation workflows easier and faster, so you can get value from automation with minimal technical effort.

To connect to services such as SharePoint or Teams, mention the site, list, or team name in your natural language prompt. For example, you can say "save the response to the 'Invoices' list on the 'ContosoCRM' SharePoint site" or "post a message in the 'Budget Planning' channel of the 'Global Finance Operations' team." If Workflows agent can't resolve a location by name, it prompts you to confirm or provide the URL to ensure accuracy and avoid errors. This helps avoid mistakes in environments where multiple sites or channels might have similar names. Workflows agent is available within Microsoft 365 Copilot and subject to your organization’s access policies. Always review and test AI-generated flows in the visual designer before enabling them. Administrators manage Workflows agent access and settings through the Microsoft 365 admin center. Training users on AI capabilities and limitations helps ensure responsible use.

content.title: What’s New in Copilot Studio: November 2025 Updates and Features
content.text:
To use HITL, open the agent-building experience and select Add tool. Choose the request for information in Copilot Studio agent flows (preview) action under the Human-in-the-loop connector, then configure fields such as the title, message, assignee, ...

To use HITL, open the agent-building experience and select Add tool. Choose the request for information in Copilot Studio agent flows (preview) action under the Human-in-the-loop connector, then configure fields such as the title, message, assignee, and inputs. The agent will automatically trigger the request whenever the workflow calls for it. Learn more about request for information. Makers can now streamline agent configuration by adding curated Action Groups from Outlook and SharePoint connectors, now in preview. Instead of identifying and configuring individual actions one by one, teams can bring in complete sets of related tools, such as “manage emails” or “manage files,” with a single selection. This makes it easier to equip agents with the capabilities they need to support common workflows across communication and content management.

This provides a faster, clearer, and more guided way to build workflow-ready agents. ... Any team that uses SharePoint knows that it can sometimes be tough to find exactly the nugget of information you need among all your content. Fortunately, if you’re using SharePoint as a knowledge source, your agents just got a lot smarter. We shipped an upgraded tenant graph grounding architecture that improves how agents retrieve and rank information across your organization. This translates into more precise, more context-aware responses, especially in content-heavy environments. On top of that, you can now filter SharePoint content using metadata like filename, owner, and last modified date. That gives you much tighter control over which documents your agents rely on when answering questions. Learn more about these features and using SharePoint as a knowledge source. Microsoft 365 Copilot now uses GPT-5 Chat when responding to prompts in agents created with Agent Builder.

This is where AI shifts from “assistant” to “producer.” Agents built inside Microsoft 365 Copilot can now create high-quality Word documents, Excel worksheets, and PowerPoint presentations using the “Generate documents, charts, and code” skillset (formerly known as Cope Interpreter). This capability is generally available everywhere Agent Builder is supported. These enhanced Office skills bring richer creation and formatting tools directly into your custom agents. Agents can generate structured documents, well-designed slides, and Excel files that incorporate charts, visuals, layouts, and other professional elements. This makes it easier for teams to create reports, summaries, plans, proposals, and analysis as part of an automated workflow. You can do all this using natural language. To try out this feature, open Agent Builder and toggle on Generate documents, charts, and code.

OneNote support also reduces the need to copy content into files or recreate notes elsewhere. Makers can simply choose the pages they want to include and let Microsoft 365 Copilot agents draw from them automatically during conversations and workflows. To add this capability: Open Agent Builder, go to Knowledge sources, choose OneNote from the file picker, and select the pages you want to include. Learn more about knowledge sources in Agent Builder. The story of November 2025 isn’t just new features. It’s a shift in how work gets designed. We’re moving toward a world where organizations don’t just automate steps—they design intelligent systems of work, where AI agents handle complexity, people apply judgment, and businesses operate with more speed, clarity, and resilience. And we’re just getting started. Check out all the updates as we ship them, as well as new features releasing in the next few months here: What’s new in Microsoft Copilot Studio.

content.title: Copilot Cowork: A new way of getting work done | Microsoft 365 Blog
content.text:
Copilot Cowork is built for that: it helps Copilot take action, not just chat. Cowork makes it easy to delegate work. Describe the outcome you want and Cowork automatically grounds the work in your emails, meetings, messages, files, and data.

Copilot Cowork is currently being tested with a limited set of customers in Research Preview, and it will be more broadly available in the Frontier program in late March 2026. Learn how to power your Frontier Transformation with Copilot and agents. ... Charles leads the design, product development, and engineering teams responsible for creating intelligent business applications and low-code platforms with generative AI. This spans products and services across Microsoft Dynamics 365, Power Platform, Copilot Studio, Copilot apps, and Nuance Enterprise. These products include business applications for Microsoft Copilot, low-code/no-code tools, industry cloud solutions, conversational AI, RPA, CRM, and ERP applications.

Over the last year, we have been pushing Copilot toward taking action. That means completing tasks, running workflows, and doing work on your behalf. Copilot Cowork is built for that: it helps Copilot take action, not just chat. Cowork makes it easy to delegate work. Describe the outcome you want and Cowork automatically grounds the work in your emails, meetings, messages, files, and data. Powered by Work IQ, Cowork draws on signals across Outlook, Teams, Excel, and the rest of Microsoft 365 so it can act with the same understanding you bring to your job. When you hand off a task to Cowork, it turns your request into a plan. The plan continues in the background, with clear checkpoints so you can confirm progress, make changes, or pause execution at any time. Cowork checks in if it needs clarification. You can see any actions that it is recommending, then approve changes before they are applied.

New product launches move fast, especially when the competitive landscape shifts midstream. With Cowork, you can delegate a launch workflow and move quickly from intent to a full-fledged approach. Cowork builds a competitive comparison in Excel, distills differentiation into a value proposition document, and generates a customer pitch deck. It can also outline milestones, owners, and next steps. This does not stop at strategy. It translates into coordinated action. You get a coherent story quickly, plus the files to back it up, without stitching together versions across tools. From there, your team can distribute it, review it, and continue improving as the launch evolves. Copilot Cowork runs within Microsoft 365’s security and governance boundaries. Identity, permissions, and compliance policies apply by default, and actions and outputs are auditable.

In the last few weeks, what has stood out most is how naturally Cowork fits into a busy day. It is easy to have a dozen tasks in flight at once, each one moving forward while you focus on what only you can do. The era of Copilot execution is here. ... That plan-to-action loop is the difference between getting an answer and getting something done. Here are four examples of Cowork turning intent into actions across Microsoft 365. Each starts with a simple ask and ends with actions that stay in your control. Across each scenario, Cowork is not just creating content—it is coordinating the work around it. Most weeks start with a packed calendar and not enough focus time. Now you can hand that triage over to Cowork. It reviews your Outlook schedule, asks what you are trying to prioritize, and flags conflicts and low-value meetings. Then it proposes changes.

핵심 정보를 충분히 확인했습니다. LG PRI HR 맥락에 맞춰 설명드리겠습니다.

---

## Copilot Workflows — 개념과 작동 방식

먼저 정확한 위치를 잡는 것이 중요합니다. Workflows는 Microsoft 365 Copilot 안에 내장된 에이전트로, 자연어로 원하는 것을 설명하면 Outlook, SharePoint, Teams, Planner에 걸친 자동화 플로우를 코드 없이 생성해주는 도구입니다. Power Automate가 커넥터와 트리거를 직접 설정해야 했다면, Workflows는 그 과정을 대화로 대체합니다.

작동 원리는 단순합니다. 원하는 자동화를 자연어로 묘사하면 Workflows 에이전트가 해당 플로우를 생성하고, 비주얼 디자이너에서 검토·수정한 뒤 실제로 켜는 구조입니다. 예를 들어 "특정 SharePoint 사이트의 'Invoices' 리스트에 응답을 저장해줘" 또는 "'Global Finance Operations' 팀의 'Budget Planning' 채널에 메시지를 올려줘"처럼 사이트명이나 채널명을 그대로 말하면 됩니다.

---

## HR 업무에서 실제로 쓸 수 있는 자동화 시나리오

트리거(시작 이벤트)와 액션(실행할 작업)의 조합으로 구성되며, 이메일 수신, 일정, Teams 메시지 등 다양한 이벤트를 트리거로 사용할 수 있습니다. LG PRI HR 업무에 대입하면 이렇게 됩니다.

**Pulse Check 관련.** "매월 첫째 주 월요일에 Teams 채널에 설문 링크 공지를 자동 발송하고, 응답이 수집되면 SharePoint 리스트에 저장해줘" — 참여율 85% KPI를 위한 리마인더 자동화가 이 한 문장으로 만들어집니다.

**교육 운영 관련.** "AX 교육 신청 이메일이 오면 신청자 정보를 Planner 태스크로 생성하고, Teams 채널에 알림을 보내줘" — 수기 집계 없이 과정별 신청 현황이 실시간으로 관리됩니다.

**보고서 배포 관련.** "SharePoint의 특정 폴더에 파일이 업로드되면 팀장 그룹에게 Outlook으로 알림 메일을 자동 발송해줘" — 월간 HR 보고서 배포 플로우를 자동화할 수 있습니다.

---

## 한 가지 중요한 조건

Workflows Agent를 사용하려면 Frontier 프로그램에 가입되어 있어야 하며, Power Platform 관리 센터의 DLP 정책에 SharePoint, Approvals, Teams, Planner, Outlook 커넥터가 포함되어 있어야 합니다. 현재 Frontier 얼리 액세스 단계이므로, IT 담당자를 통해 M365 라이선스 수준과 Frontier 프로그램 접근 가능 여부를 먼저 확인하는 것이 선행 조건입니다. 이 조건이 갖춰지면 코딩 없이 HR 구성원 누구든 자연어만으로 자동화 워크플로우를 직접 만들 수 있는 환경이 됩니다. 그 자체가 Layer 2 역량 개발의 실질적 진입점이 될 수 있습니다.

**접근 조건.** Frontier 프로그램 가입이 필요하고, Power Platform 관리 센터에서 SharePoint·Approvals·Teams·Planner·Outlook 커넥터가 DLP 정책에 포함되어 있어야 합니다. 현재 일반 배포 전 얼리 액세스 단계입니다.

**현실적 한계.** M365 커넥터만 지원하며 외부 서비스 연동은 불가하고, 영어로만 작동하며, AI가 생성한 플로우는 반드시 비주얼 디자이너에서 검토 후 사용해야 합니다. 한국어 지원이 아직 없다는 점이 LG PRI 현장 도입에서 실질적 장벽이 될 수 있습니다.

결론적으로 기능 자체는 실재하지만, 지금 당장 LG PRI 구성원이 바로 쓸 수 있는 상태는 아닙니다. IT팀을 통해 M365 라이선스 등급과 Frontier 프로그램 가입 여부를 확인하는 것이 먼저입니다. 한국어 지원이 추가되는 시점이 실질적 도입 타이밍이 될 것 같습니다.

content.text: 좋은 질문입니다. 저도 확인이 필요합니다.

# FAQ for Workflows agent

Feedback

Summarize this article for me

## In this article

These frequently asked questions describe the AI impact of Workflows agent, the AI assistant embedded in Microsoft 365 Copilot that helps you build automation flows using natural language.

## What is Workflows agent?

Workflows agent is an AI-powered assistant within Microsoft 365 Copilot that helps you create, edit, and manage automation flows across Microsoft 365 apps such as Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, and Planner. You describe what you want to automate in natural language, and Workflows agent generates the corresponding flow for you. It also helps answer questions about your flows and provides documentation links as needed.

## What are Workflows agent’s capabilities?

Workflows agent can:

* Create new flows from scratch based on your descriptions.
* Edit existing flows according to your instructions.
* Answer questions about the flow you’re working on.
* Provide descriptions of your flows in plain language.
* Offer helpful documentation links about Microsoft 365 automation.
* Filter out unrelated questions.
* Suggest when to save or test your flow.

## What is Workflows agent's intended use?

Workflows agent is designed to make building automation workflows easier and faster, so you can get value from automation with minimal technical effort.

## How is Workflows agent evaluated? What metrics are used to measure performance?

Microsoft tracks several key metrics to ensure Workflows agent performs well and provides a positive experience. These include uptime (to ensure availability), user feedback through thumbs up/down on AI outputs, and reports of biased or inappropriate responses. This feedback helps maintain fairness, compliance, and quality.

## What are the limitations of Workflows agent? How can users minimize these limitations?

* Workflows agent supports only a limited set of Microsoft 365 connectors; non-Microsoft and custom connectors aren't supported.
* Workflows agent can't currently fix errors in existing flows automatically.
* Since Workflows agent relies on natural language input in chat to specify locations like SharePoint sites or Teams channels, it might occasionally identify the wrong site or channel if the names are ambiguous or similar.
* AI-generated flows should always be reviewed and tested before use in production.
* Workflows agent is only available in English.

To minimize these limitations:

* Provide clear and precise names when referencing sites, lists, or teams.
* Review and test all flows before enabling them.

## How does Workflows agent connect to services like SharePoint or Teams?

To connect to services such as SharePoint or Teams, mention the site, list, or team name in your natural language prompt. For example, you can say "save the response to the 'Invoices' list on the 'ContosoCRM' SharePoint site" or "post a message in the 'Budget Planning' channel of the 'Global Finance Operations' team."

If Workflows agent can't resolve a location by name, it prompts you to confirm or provide the URL to ensure accuracy and avoid errors. This helps avoid mistakes in environments where multiple sites or channels might have similar names.

## What operational factors and settings allow for effective and responsible use?

* Workflows agent is available within Microsoft 365 Copilot and subject to your organization’s access policies.
* Always review and test AI-generated flows in the visual designer before enabling them.
* Administrators manage Workflows agent access and settings through the Microsoft 365 admin center.
* Training users on AI capabilities and limitations helps ensure responsible use.

## How can administrators manage Workflows agent in their organization?

Administrators can enable or disable Workflows agent for users through the Microsoft 365 admin center under Copilot settings. They can assign access to all users, specific groups, or disable it completely. Usage can be monitored to maintain security and compliance.

content.text: 여기서 workflow는 ms 제품이 아니라, 실제 워크플로우를 ai기반으로 재설계하는 형태가 맞을 것 같은데

chat_messages.text:
맞습니다. 완전히 다른 개념이었네요.

돌아보면 앞서 나온 "Workflows"는 MS 제품명으로 잘못 연결된 것이고, 원래 전략에서 말한 "워크플로우 재설계"는 **구성원이 자신의 반복 업무를 AI 기반으로 직접 재설계하는 행위** 자체를 의미하는 것이 맞습니다.

두 개념의 차이를 명확히 하면 이렇습니다.

| | MS Copilot Workflows | AI 기반 워크플로우 재설계 |
|---|---|---|
| 성격 | MS 제품 기능명 | 방법론·역량 개발 활동 |
| 주체 | IT/관리자가 설정 | 구성원 본인이 설계 |
| 결과물 | 자동화 플로우 스크립트 | 업무 방식의 변화 |
| 도구 | M365 내부 커넥터 | Copilot, Antigravity, Lovable 등 자유롭게 |

그러면 Layer 1의 "Workflows" 노드 라벨도 수정이 필요합니다. 지금 다이어그램에서 Workflows라고 표시된 것을 **"업무 재설계"** 혹은 **"AI 침투 내재화"** 같은 표현으로 바꾸는 게 맞겠죠.

수정된 다이어그램을 다시 그릴까요? 아니면 워크플로우 재설계 방법론 자체를 먼저 개념화할까요?

content.text:
맞습니다. 완전히 다른 개념이었네요.

돌아보면 앞서 나온 "Workflows"는 MS 제품명으로 잘못 연결된 것이고, 원래 전략에서 말한 "워크플로우 재설계"는 **구성원이 자신의 반복 업무를 AI 기반으로 직접 재설계하는 행위** 자체를 의미하는 것이 맞습니다.

두 개념의 차이를 명확히 하면 이렇습니다.

| | MS Copilot Workflows | AI 기반 워크플로우 재설계 |
|---|---|---|
| 성격 | MS 제품 기능명 | 방법론·역량 개발 활동 |
| 주체 | IT/관리자가 설정 | 구성원 본인이 설계 |
| 결과물 | 자동화 플로우 스크립트 | 업무 방식의 변화 |
| 도구 | M365 내부 커넥터 | Copilot, Antigravity, Lovable 등 자유롭게 |

그러면 Layer 1의 "Workflows" 노드 라벨도 수정이 필요합니다. 지금 다이어그램에서 Workflows라고 표시된 것을 **"업무 재설계"** 혹은 **"AI 침투 내재화"** 같은 표현으로 바꾸는 게 맞겠죠.

수정된 다이어그램을 다시 그릴까요? 아니면 워크플로우 재설계 방법론 자체를 먼저 개념화할까요?

chat_messages.text: 진행

content.text: 진행

chat_messages.text:
```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```

다이어그램 수정 완료입니다. 이제 "워크플로우 재설계"가 MS 제품이 아니라 **구성원이 자신의 업무를 AI로 다시 그리는 행위**로 명확하게 자리잡혔습니다.

그런데 이 변경이 실은 꽤 중요한 개념적 전환을 열어줍니다. Layer 2의 핵심 결과물이 "앱을 만드는 것"이 아니라 **"자기 업무의 Before/After를 설계하는 것"** 이라면, 실습 구조도 달라져야 합니다.

구체적으로 말하면, 구성원이 Lovable이나 Antigravity를 배우는 것 자체가 목적이 아니라, 본인의 반복 업무 하나를 골라서 그것을 AI 기반으로 어떻게 바꿀 수 있는지 설계하는 10주 프로젝트 형태가 되는 것이죠. 도구는 그 설계를 실현하는 수단일 뿐입니다.

이 방향으로 실습 설계와 평가 기준을 구체화할까요?

다이어그램 수정 완료입니다. 이제 "워크플로우 재설계"가 MS 제품이 아니라 **구성원이 자신의 업무를 AI로 다시 그리는 행위**로 명확하게 자리잡혔습니다.

그런데 이 변경이 실은 꽤 중요한 개념적 전환을 열어줍니다. Layer 2의 핵심 결과물이 "앱을 만드는 것"이 아니라 **"자기 업무의 Before/After를 설계하는 것"** 이라면, 실습 구조도 달라져야 합니다.

구체적으로 말하면, 구성원이 Lovable이나 Antigravity를 배우는 것 자체가 목적이 아니라, 본인의 반복 업무 하나를 골라서 그것을 AI 기반으로 어떻게 바꿀 수 있는지 설계하는 10주 프로젝트 형태가 되는 것이죠. 도구는 그 설계를 실현하는 수단일 뿐입니다.

이 방향으로 실습 설계와 평가 기준을 구체화할까요?

chat_messages.text: AI 기반 업무 재설계란 무엇인가요?

content.text: AI 기반 업무 재설계란 무엇인가요?

chat_messages.text:
에세이 형식으로 풀겠습니다. 이 질문은 단순한 방법론 설명이 아니라 CSP가 EXG 팀에서 구현하려는 것의 철학적 기반에 닿아있기 때문입니다.

---

## AI 기반 업무 재설계란 무엇인가

### 출발점 — "어떻게 하는가"가 아니라 "무엇이 바뀌는가"

업무 재설계라는 말을 들으면 대부분 프로세스 다이어그램을 떠올립니다. As-Is를 그리고, To-Be를 그리고, 갭을 채우는 것. 하지만 AI 기반 업무 재설계는 그것과 본질적으로 다릅니다.

기존의 프로세스 재설계는 **같은 일을 더 빠르게** 하는 것이 목표였습니다. AI 기반 재설계는 질문 자체가 다릅니다. **이 일을 내가 해야 하는가.** 더 정확히는, **이 일의 어떤 부분이 인간 고유의 판단을 필요로 하는가.** 그 경계를 다시 긋는 것이 재설계의 본질입니다.

---

### 세 가지 전환으로 보는 재설계의 층위

업무 재설계는 깊이에 따라 세 층위로 나뉩니다.

**첫 번째 층위 — 자동화(Automation).** 반복적이고 규칙이 명확한 업무를 AI에게 위임합니다. 회의록 요약, 이메일 초안, 데이터 정리처럼 "매번 비슷하게 해왔던 것들"이 여기에 속합니다. MS Copilot이 Layer 1에서 하는 일이 바로 이것입니다. 이 층위의 재설계는 시간을 돌려줍니다. 하지만 일의 성격 자체는 바뀌지 않습니다.

**두 번째 층위 — 증강(Augmentation).** 인간이 하던 판단의 질을 AI가 끌어올립니다. 채용 면접 평가지를 작성할 때 AI가 편향 패턴을 실시간으로 짚어주거나, 조직 진단 결과를 해석할 때 AI가 유사 사례와 비교 분석을 제공하는 것이 여기에 해당합니다. 이 층위에서는 인간의 역할이 바뀝니다. 정보를 처리하는 사람에서 **맥락을 판단하는 사람**으로.

**세 번째 층위 — 재창조(Reinvention).** 업무의 존재 이유 자체를 다시 묻습니다. 매월 작성하던 HR 보고서가 실시간 대시보드로 대체된다면, 보고서를 작성하는 업무는 사라지고 **인사이트를 해석하고 의사결정에 연결하는 업무**가 새로 생깁니다. 이 층위의 재설계는 직무 정의 자체를 바꿉니다.

LG PRI EXG 팀이 2026년에 달성하려는 것은 대부분의 구성원이 최소 첫 번째 층위를 완전히 체화하고, 핵심 구성원이 두 번째 층위에 진입하는 것입니다. 세 번째 층위는 CSP 개인 프로젝트와 EXG 전용 Agent 개발이 담당합니다.

---

### 재설계의 실제 방법 — Before/After 매핑

추상적인 이야기를 구체적으로 만드는 가장 효과적인 방법은 **한 가지 업무를 골라 해부하는 것**입니다.

구성원이 매주 반복하는 업무 하나를 선택합니다. 예를 들어 "팀장 회의 전 자료 준비". 그리고 다음 네 가지 질문을 던집니다.

지금 이 업무에서 내가 실제로 쓰는 시간은 얼마인가. 그 시간 중 판단이 필요한 부분과 정보 수집·정리에 쓰이는 부분은 각각 얼마인가. 정보 수집·정리 부분을 AI가 대신한다면 나는 무엇을 더 잘할 수 있는가. 그리고 AI가 틀렸을 때 내가 그것을 감지할 수 있는가.

이 네 번째 질문이 가장 중요합니다. AI 기반 재설계에서 인간이 잃어서는 안 되는 것은 **감지 능력**입니다. AI가 생성한 결과물이 맞는지 틀렸는지를 판단할 수 있는 도메인 이해. 그것이 없으면 자동화는 효율이 아니라 리스크가 됩니다.

---

### CSP 맥락에서의 의미

"심리학이 코드가 되고, 철학이 알고리즘이 된다"는 CSP의 자기 정의를 업무 재설계에 대입하면 이렇게 됩니다. HR 전문가로서 쌓아온 17년의 도메인 이해가 **AI가 틀렸을 때 감지하는 능력**의 원천입니다. 이것은 AI가 대체할 수 없는 영역입니다.

동시에 그 도메인 이해를 시스템으로 구현하는 능력 — 이것이 재설계의 실행력입니다. Pulse Check 시스템이 단순한 설문 도구가 아니라 조직 심리의 인과 구조를 담은 진단 플랫폼이 될 수 있는 이유가 여기에 있습니다. 그리고 EXG 구성원이 자신의 업무를 재설계할 때, 그 과정을 안내할 수 있는 사람이 CSP여야 하는 이유도 같습니다.

결국 AI 기반 업무 재설계란 도구를 배우는 것이 아니라, **내가 하는 일의 어느 부분이 나여야 하는지를 다시 정의하는 작업**입니다. 그 정의가 먼저 있어야 도구 선택이 의미를 갖습니다.

결국 AI 기반 업무 재설계란 도구를 배우는 것이 아니라, **내가 하는 일의 어느 부분이 나여야 하는지를 다시 정의하는 작업**입니다. 그 정의가 먼저 있어야 도구 선택이 의미를 갖습니다.

This block is not supported on your current device yet.

흥미로운 사실이 하나 나왔습니다. Word, Excel, PowerPoint Agent는 Anthropic의 Claude 모델을 단독으로 사용합니다. MS Copilot 안에서 Claude가 작동하는 구조입니다. LG PRI 맥락에서 이미 Claude를 쓰고 있는 CSP 입장에서는 익숙한 엔진이 사내 표준 도구 안으로 들어오는 셈입니다.

작동 방식을 앱별로 정리하면 이렇습니다.

---

### 핵심 구조 — "1회 생성"에서 "반복 편집"으로

기존 Copilot이 프롬프트 하나에 결과물 하나를 뱉는 방식이었다면, Agent Mode는 다릅니다. Agent Mode는 사용자의 프롬프트에 응답하면서 동시에 파일에 직접 변경을 가하고, 그 과정에서 추론을 거치며 능동적으로 수정하는 방식으로 작동합니다. Copilot이 문서 옆에 앉아 함께 편집하는 것에 가깝습니다.

흐름은 두 단계로 나뉩니다. Copilot Chat에서 자연어로 초안을 만드는 "채팅 우선 생성" 단계, 그리고 앱 안에서 Agent Mode로 정밀하게 다듬는 "앱 우선 편집" 단계입니다.

---

**Word.** 청중, 톤, 테마 같은 질문을 먼저 던지고, 완성도 높은 문서를 생성한 뒤 대화를 통해 섹션 추가·톤 변경·내용 확장을 반복합니다. 이메일·회의·파일 같은 Microsoft Graph 데이터를 불러와 실제 조직 맥락을 반영할 수 있습니다. HR 보고서, 제안서, 가이드 같은 장문 문서 작성에 가장 효과적입니다.

**Excel.** 멀티스텝 데이터 분석을 수행하고, 수식 작성·차트 생성·새 시트 생성을 계획하고 실행합니다. Copilot의 추론 과정도 단계별로 확인할 수 있습니다. Pulse Check 결과 분석이나 AX 성숙도 데이터를 다룰 때 수식을 직접 짤 필요 없이 "분기별 Well-Being 점수 변화를 차트로 보여줘"라고 말하면 됩니다.

**PowerPoint.** 주제·청중·구조에 대한 명확화 질문에 답하면 완성된 프레젠테이션을 생성하고, 조직의 템플릿과 브랜드 색상·레이아웃·이미지를 자동으로 맞춥니다. EX Intelligence 경영진 보고 자료처럼 반복적으로 만드는 덱에 바로 활용할 수 있습니다.

---

### LG PRI 현장 적용 시 실질적 조건

관리자 별도 설정 없이 Word·Excel·PPT의 Tools 메뉴 또는 "@" 입력으로 바로 접근 가능하며, 2026년 1월부터 3월 사이 순차 배포 중입니다. 단, Anthropic을 서브프로세서로 활성화해야 작동하며, 현재 EU 데이터 경계 및 일부 국가별 처리 약정에서는 제외되어 있습니다. IT 관리자가 테넌트 설정에서 Anthropic 모델을 허용했는지 확인이 먼저입니다.

조건만 갖춰지면 구성원이 별도 교육 없이 기존 Word·Excel·PPT 안에서 바로 경험할 수 있는 기능입니다. Layer 1 침투 전략에서 가장 마찰이 적은 진입점입니다.

content.title: Introducing Word, Excel, and PowerPoint Agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot | Microsoft Community Hub
content.text:
This approach allows for these Agents to use the same Word, Excel and PowerPoint skills used in Agent Mode in the apps, to generate rich and complex documents, workbooks, and presentations now within Copilot Chat.

The new Word, Excel, and PowerPoint Agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot are dedicated agents that help users create documents, spreadsheets, and presentations—directly in chat. Built on Microsoft’s deep expertise in productivity, these agents use the latest reasoning models to deliver structured, high-quality outputs. With dedicated Word, Excel, and PowerPoint Agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot, users can now start projects in Copilot Chat—using natural language prompts to quickly generate documents, spreadsheets, and presentations. These agents don’t just create; they guide users with clarifying questions, helping shape outputs that match specific goals and use cases. This chat-first approach makes it easy to get started, whether you’re drafting a strategic plan, analyzing data, or building a presentation. Once the initial draft is ready, Copilot makes it seamless to move into Word, Excel, or PowerPoint for deeper editing and customization.

Inside each app, Copilot continues to assist, help refine content, apply formatting, and it’s easy to continue collaborating with others. Agent Mode in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint extends Copilot’s capabilities within each application, enabling you to make direct edits and changes with powerful, app-specific features—unlocking new Copilot interactivity and action that just weren’t possible before. Notably, Agent Mode in PowerPoint is new to Frontier, bringing enhanced creation and editing capabilities to presentations for the first time. This integrated flow—from chat-first creation to app-first refinement—means users get the best of both worlds: rapid ideation and easy editing, all powered by Copilot. Just as we’ve seen the emergence of coding agents in Software Engineering, our goal was to extend that paradigm to Office, so that our Agents can orchestrate time consuming, complex tasks on behalf of the user to generate meaningful, high-fidelity Office artifacts.

To that end, a key bet we took was on an agentic harness, which allowed us to model these complex tasks at scale, leveraging the power of various underlying AI models. This approach allows for these Agents to use the same Word, Excel and PowerPoint skills used in Agent Mode in the apps, to generate rich and complex documents, workbooks, and presentations now within Copilot Chat. AI based code-optimized reasoning allows the agents to plan, validate, and refine through multiple stages, producing results that are both robust and reliable. Combined with the Office product architecture and Microsoft’s enterprise-grade infrastructure and graph, this approach represents a new milestone in AI-driven co-creation. Early results are promising. By applying our standard evaluation processes and quality metrics, we observe that output quality and relevance are comparable to other agentic systems.

By creating documents through headless versions of the Word, Excel, PowerPoint apps themselves, we ensure high-quality, fully compatible files with styles, themes, and formulas that open and co-author seamlessly in Office. Using official endpoints also prevents unsafe elements like macros or external links while also preserving key data protection elements like sensitivity labels, unlike many AI generators that stitch XML files together. Our approach lets Office itself build the final artifact, leveraging decades of engineering for reliability and security. Enterprise grounding (via Work IQ) and context: A key differentiator is the ability to ground creation in enterprise data. Integrated within the Copilot ecosystem, our agents securely retrieve organizational information such as notes, emails, or shared documents to enrich outputs with real context.
